title: Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha
intro: Read Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha in English and Hindi. This sacred story of Goddess Parvati’s devotion and penance describes the importance of faith, marital happiness, and the blessings of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.
description:
Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha

Long ago, King Himavan and Queen Maina lived on Mount Himalaya with their daughter Parvati.

From childhood, Goddess Parvati was a devoted follower of Lord Shiva.
She had accepted Lord Shiva as her husband with complete faith and devotion.

Goddess Parvati decided to perform severe penance to attain Lord Shiva as her husband.

She lived in the forest for many years and performed intense meditation.
She continued her penance without caring about heat, cold, or rain.

The glory of Goddess Parvati's devotion spread throughout the three worlds.
Even the gods and divine beings were amazed by her dedication.

One day, Sage Narada visited King Himavan.
He brought a marriage proposal from Lord Vishnu for Goddess Parvati.

King Himavan was very happy to receive such a noble proposal.
He considered accepting the marriage proposal.

When Goddess Parvati came to know about this.
She became deeply saddened.

She told her friends,
"I have accepted Lord Shiva as my husband."

"I cannot marry anyone else."

Goddess Parvati's friends took her to a dense forest.
They wanted her to complete her penance without any disturbance.

On the day of Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya, Goddess Parvati observed a strict fast to attain Lord Shiva.

She created an idol of Lord Shiva using clay.
She worshipped the idol with complete devotion and rituals.

She spent the entire day and night meditating on Lord Shiva.
She prayed to Him with unwavering faith.

Pleased with Goddess Parvati's intense penance and pure devotion,
Lord Shiva appeared before her.

Lord Shiva blessed Goddess Parvati and told her that her penance had been successful.

Lord Shiva accepted Goddess Parvati as his wife.

After this, Goddess Parvati returned to her father's home.
King Himavan was also very happy to marry his daughter to Lord Shiva.

In time, the divine marriage of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati was celebrated with great joy.

The gods and goddesses attended this sacred wedding and blessed Goddess Parvati.

Because of Goddess Parvati's dedication, patience, and devotion,
she received Lord Shiva as her husband.

Since then, the importance of Hartalika Teej Vrat became famous throughout the world.

It is believed that women who observe Hartalika Teej Vrat with faith and devotion.
Receive the blessings of Goddess Parvati for an unbroken marital bond and a happy married life.

Married women observe this vrat for the long life, good health, and prosperity of their husbands and families.

Unmarried girls also worship Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati to pray for a suitable life partner.

On Hartalika Teej, women observe a strict fast and worship Lord Shiva, Goddess Parvati, and Lord Ganesha.

It is a tradition to create idols of Shiva and Parvati from clay and worship them on this day.

Women wear traditional attire, perform sixteen adornments, and offer items of marital prosperity to Goddess Parvati.

Devotees stay awake at night and listen to the Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha.

It is believed that observing this vrat brings love, trust, and happiness in married life.

Goddess Parvati blesses her devotees with strength, patience, and good fortune.

Those who worship Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati with a pure heart.
Receive happiness, peace, and auspiciousness in their lives.

Listening to the Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha and performing the puja properly brings the blessings of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.

Goddess Parvati fulfills the wishes of her devotees and protects their families.

With this faith and devotion, women observe the sacred Hartalika Teej Vrat every year.

Har Har Mahadev.
Jai Mata Parvati.

faq.a1: Hartalika Teej Vrat is a sacred fast dedicated to Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati. Married women observe it for the long life of their husbands and a happy married life.
faq.q2: Which deities are worshipped during Hartalika Teej?
faq.a2: Hartalika Teej Vrat is dedicated to Goddess Parvati and Lord Shiva. Devotees remember Goddess Parvati’s penance and her devotion to attain Lord Shiva.
faq.q3: When should the Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha be recited?
faq.a3: The Hartalika Teej Vrat Katha is traditionally listened to during the puja, Shiva-Parvati worship, and night prayers.
faq.a4: Married women mainly observe this vrat for the long life, happiness, and prosperity of their husbands. Unmarried girls also observe it to pray for a suitable life partner.
faq.q5: What are the benefits of observing Hartalika Teej Vrat?
faq.a5: It is believed that observing this vrat brings marital happiness, good fortune, family peace, and the blessings of Goddess Parvati.
faq.q6: What offerings are made during Hartalika Teej Puja?
faq.a6: Devotees offer flowers, fruits, Bilva leaves, incense, lamps, clothes, marital items, and sweets to Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.
faq.q7: Is fasting without water observed on Hartalika Teej?
faq.a7: Traditionally, many women observe a Nirjala (without water) fast on Hartalika Teej, but fasting should be followed according to one’s health and ability.
faq.q8: What is the significance of Hartalika Teej?
faq.a8: Hartalika Teej represents Goddess Parvati’s devotion, intense penance, and her divine union with Lord Shiva. It symbolizes love, faith, and marital happiness.
